user_query:
I need a small, runnable script or CLI program that helps me produce a weekly update on modern construction techniques for our projects. Please implement it so it runs in a normal local workspace without special credentials and generates these artifacts under the workspace:

Required inputs (read these local files):
- input/projects.csv (projects with stage and technologies)
- input/tech_keywords.json (categories and the keywords to look for)

Required web content (download and save raw HTML):
- U.S. Department of Energy (energy.gov) — Building Technologies Office page titled "Advanced Building Construction (ABC) Initiative" (found under an energy.gov path containing "eere/buildings" or "building-technologies-office"). Save to out/web/raw/doe_abc.html.
- National Institute of Building Sciences (nibs.org) — a page whose title includes "Off-Site Construction Council" (the council’s main page). Save to out/web/raw/nibs_oscc.html.
If the exact page cannot be resolved, download the closest official page on the same domain whose <title> or <h1> contains the specified phrase, and record the final URL used. Do not use paywalled content or APIs; fetch public pages only. Avoid hardcoding page text—extract from what you actually download at runtime.

Processing and outputs:
1) Parse input/projects.csv and input/tech_keywords.json. The technologies column in projects.csv may contain multiple items.
2) From the downloaded pages, extract readable text and search case-insensitively for the keywords from input/tech_keywords.json. For each match, capture:
   - source_title (page title if available)
   - source_domain (e.g., energy.gov, nibs.org)
   - url (the final URL you used)
   - category (from tech_keywords.json)
   - keyword (the exact matched term)
   - context (a short snippet around the match, e.g., ~150–300 chars)
   - retrieved_at (ISO timestamp)
   Deduplicate exact duplicate entries per (url, keyword, context) combination.
   Save all matches as JSON to out/data/web_matches.json (a list of objects with the fields above).
3) Using projects.csv, map each project’s technologies to keyword categories found in web_matches.json. Produce out/data/project_relevance.csv with columns:
   - project_name
   - stage
   - matched_categories (semicolon-separated)
   - matched_keywords (semicolon-separated)
   - sources (semicolon-separated list of source_domain values that had matches for those categories)
4) Compose a concise Markdown weekly update at out/report/weekly_update.md with the following sections:
   - Title line with today’s date
   - Highlights by Technology: for each category with at least one match, include the category name, a count of matches, and 1–3 representative bullets with keyword and brief context plus source_domain
   - Project Relevance: list projects that have any matched categories, with a one-line summary of relevant categories and sources
   - Sources and Retrieval: list each source_title with its url and retrieved_at
If no matches are found, still generate the report with those sections present and a clear note that no relevant signals were detected this cycle.

General requirements:
- Create any missing directories under out/ as needed.
- Use UTF-8 for file I/O.
- Treat keyword matching as case-insensitive and robust to simple punctuation.
- All outputs must be derived from the local inputs and the downloaded pages; do not hardcode the web content.

Input paths are fixed as provided; output paths must be exactly:
- out/web/raw/doe_abc.html
- out/web/raw/nibs_oscc.html
- out/data/web_matches.json
- out/data/project_relevance.csv
- out/report/weekly_update.md

Let me know how to run your script in a README note at the top of the report. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
